label: Sinaloa Pacific Trafficking Route — Culiacán to Tijuana
notes: Primary cocaine and methamphetamine corridor used by Sinaloa Cartel from production areas in Sinaloa to the Tijuana border crossing. One of the most contested trafficking routes of the Calderón era.

label: Gulf Cartel I-35 Corridor — Matamoros to Nuevo Laredo
notes: The Gulf Cartel's primary drug corridor running along the Texas border, connecting Matamoros (Gulf HQ) through Reynosa to Nuevo Laredo — the gateway to the I-35 corridor into the US interior.

label: Zetas Territorial Expansion — Nuevo Laredo to Veracruz
notes: Following the 2010 split from the Gulf Cartel, Los Zetas expanded their territorial control southward from Tamaulipas through San Luis Potosí and Hidalgo to Veracruz, establishing extortion and kidnapping operations across a vast new area.

label: Pacific Cocaine Route — Lázaro Cárdenas to Culiacán
notes: Colombian cocaine entered Mexico's Pacific coast through the port of Lázaro Cárdenas, Michoacán — controlled at various times by La Familia Michoacana and Knights Templar — before being transshipped northward to Sinaloa Cartel for US distribution.

label: BLO Sonora Corridor — Hermosillo to Nogales
notes: Beltrán Leyva Organization trafficking corridor through Sonora to the Nogales border crossing after their 2008 split from Sinaloa. The BLO contested this corridor with Sinaloa throughout 2008–2010.
